methodology: Hand-decomposed by the implementer from apps/evals/results/baseline_v1.jsonl synthesis_text field. LLM-free: each claim was read and decomposed manually. One primary claim extracted per synthesis record (the most concrete, actionable statement). Scoped-out dimensions include phrasing, interpretation, and timbre -- these appear frequently in the teacher's prose but have no deterministic physical oracle.

sample_claims.proposition: Your pedaling created exactly the kind of luminous, breathing resonance the Nocturne needs
The melodic dynamics need to ebb and swell like a voice
Your pedaling let the harmonies bloom and linger
The melody is singing too evenly without dynamic swell
Your pedaling was balanced without blurring the chromatic lines
The right-hand chromatic runs need more evenness in note weighting
You are reaching for the pedal to connect broken chords
The pedaling should lift at harmony shifts to avoid blur
Your pedaling was singing throughout the session
The dynamic range from pp to ff is not fully explored
Your pedaling was doing beautiful work in this piece
The phrases feel too evenly lit without dynamic ebb and flow
The pedal creates warmth and resonance in the Nocturne
The dynamic landscape lacks long arching swells and sudden hushes
The bass notes breathe underneath the triplets due to pedaling
Pedal should clear and reset at harmony shifts
Your pedaling let harmonies breathe and bloom in a Chopinesque way
The music is telling a story rather than just playing notes
Your pedaling sustained the moonlight quality with lifted moments
The triplets are starting to blur together in some sections
Your pedaling kept the fast chromatic runs clear without muddiness
The dynamic architecture of the etude needs more inner voice variety
The musical shaping brings a sense of telling a story
The phrases feel like a single long breath in the opening theme
The musical contour lacks direction in the ornamental passages
There is a real sense of inhabiting the piece rather than just playing notes

The phrasing arcs need more commitment to swell and release

There was a slight rush in the ornamental passages
